title: NVTS Bets on AI Data Centers: Will This Fuel Long-Term Growth?
text: Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) is strategically repositioning away from lower-margin consumer products toward high-power applications, particularly AI data centers. The company specializes in gallium nitride (GaN) and silicon carbide (SiC) semiconductor solutions that enhance efficiency and power density in data center systems. NVTS has secured recognition as a power selector partner in NVIDIA's next-generation 800-volt DC AI factory ecosystem, a significant endorsement positioning the company in a rapidly expanding market. The company is currently sampling mid-voltage GaN devices and SiC modules targeting AI server infrastructure. The article notes that meaningful revenues from AI data centers will not show up before 2027, with 2026 characterized as a transition year featuring modest shipments for traditional server power supplies. NVTS faces formidable competition from Wolfspeed (WOLF) and ON Semiconductor (ON), both aggressively developing SiC solutions for data center power infrastructure. NVTS stock gained 51.8% over six months with a forward P/S ratio of 61.19X (versus industry average of 8.72X), 2026 earnings estimate of -$0.19 per share, and Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).
